articles.title: Sonnagar - Dankuni
articles.extracted_text: Sonnagar- Dankuni Section The Sonnagar–Dankuni section, originally part of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), spans approximately 538 km and traverses three states: Bihar, Jharkhand, and West Bengal. It passes through two zonal railways: East Central Railway (ECR) and Eastern Railway (ER). In 2022, the Sonnagar–Andal section was sanctioned as a multi-tracking project under the EPC mode and subsequently handed over to ECR and ER. The Andal–Dankuni section, covering 163 km, lies entirely within the state of West Bengal and falls under the jurisdiction of Eastern Railway. This section includes two junction stations (New Khana and New Dankuni), two crossing stations (New Rajbandh and New Jaugram), and one cabin at Andal East. As of now, approximately 97% of the required land for this section is under possession.

articles.title: New Corridor
articles.extracted_text: The proposed corridor stretching 1080 km traverses through three states of India, namely West Bengal, Orissa, and Andhra Pradesh. Mostly runs parallel to the existing Kharagpur-Vijayawada coastal railway line. It also passes over three zonal railways, namely South Eastern, East Coast and South-Central Railway. The DPR for this corridor is likely to be completed by Dec’22. Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to East Coast Corridor in PIA are mines of Coal, Iron ore, Thermal Power Plants, Ports, Fertilizers, POL, CONCOR ICDs, FCI godowns, etc. 2. East West Corridor :- The proposed East-West DFC (EWDFC) from Bhusaval to Dankuni including spur line from Rajkharswan to Andal passes through five states namely West Bangal, Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, and Maharashtra. The length of the mainline is 1551 RKM and the length of the spur line is 187 RKM (Total: 1738 RKM). This corridor has further been extended from Bhusaval to Palghar. The DPR for this corridor is planned to be completed by Mar’23. Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to East-West Corridor in project influence area (PIA) are Mines of Coal, Iron ore, bauxite, Manganese, Ferro Alloys, Steel Pants, Ports – major and minor, Fertilizers, Thermal Power Plants, Petroleum Oil Lubricants (POL), Cement plants, Container Corporation (CONCOR) Inland Container Deports (ICDs), Food Corporation of India (FCI) godowns etc. 3. North-South Corridor :- Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to North-South Corridor in Project influence area (PIA) are Mines of Coal, Cement plants, Thermal Power Plants (TPP), Petroleum Oil Lubricants (POL), Container Corporation (CONCOR) Inland Container Deports (ICDs), Food Corporation of India (FCI) godowns, Fertilizers etc.

DELHI (metro Rail News): The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) is planning to introduce in-train audio commercials over the Red (Line 1), Yellow (Line 2), Blue (Line 3), and Magenta Lines (Line 8), which will streamline the non-fare revenue and provide long-term sustainability to the Delhi Metro system. Against this backdrop, DMRC floated a tender for the licensing of audio advertisement slots in selected trains in the Metro Lines 1, 2, 3, and 8. Here is the duration of audio advertisements across the four metro lines. Delhi Metro earned a total revenue of ₹4,600.62 Crore in the FY 2024-25. Non-Fare revenue from commercial advertisements, parkings, rents from commercial spaces within the metro premises, station box area, fines and feeder services constituted around 20 percent of this revenue, while the primary resource still remained the ticketing revenue with around 80 percent contribution. This shows an excessive dependence on the ticketing revenue. Non-fare revenue is significant for the metro system to diversify its income sources and attain financial stability. It strengthens the overall financial structure of the metro system. For comparison, the Hong Kong metro, known globally for its financial sustainability, earns more than half of its total revenue from non-fare sources. WEST BENGAL (Metro Rail News): Jupiter Wagons Limited has announced a major project involving the manufacturing of India’s premium train, Vande Bharat and Metro coaches. The company is setting up a new manufacturing plant at Hooghly in West Bengal that will boost the production of Vande Bharat and Metro coaches. Upon its establishment, the plant will stand out as West Bengal’s second railway rolling stock manufacturing facility after Titagarh’s Uttarpara plant. The chairman of the company, Mr ML Lohia said that the new plant where they are going to manufacture Vande Bharat, Metro and other passenger train coaches will come up at Hooghly district. He said that the exact location of the facility will be informed later, as reported by Times of India (TOI). He further informed that the Hooghly plant was among several projects in their pipeline as the company targets a revenue of ₹5,000 Crore in 2027-’28 compared to ₹3,000 Crore currently. Moreover, the company envisions a revenue of ₹10,000 Crore by 2028-’29. Earlier, Titagarh Rail Systems Limited had started a facility to manufacture railway rolling stock at Uttarpara in West Bengal in collaboration with BHEL. The development strengthens India’s position as a rolling stock manufacturing hub. Jupiter Wagons is also setting up another 100 acres facility at Khordha in Odisha to manufacture integrated axles and wheels in collaboration with a German company. The facility is expected to start Axle manufacturing by December 2026, followed by the production of wheels by 2027. The chairman informed that there will be foreign investments in all upcoming projects, and 50 percent of the output will be targeted for export. This project is expected to strengthen India’s position as an exporter in the global market. Jupiter Wagons Limited is an Indian manufacturer of mobility infrastructure. It operates 13 manufacturing units across the country. The company is strengthening India’s mobility sector by providing solutions and technologies for the sector’s growth. NEW DELHI (Metro Rail News): Every new train in the Northeast Frontier Railway tells the story of how the region has moved closer to the rest of the country. The region now has its first Mainline Electric Multiple Unit (MEMU) train, which offers faster, cleaner, and more comfortable rail travel between Tripura and Assam. The new MEMU train will operate between Agartala and Karimganj. It will replace the previous Diesel Electric Multiple Unit (DEMU) service. The Northeast Frontier Railway (NFR) will run and manage the train. Tripura Chief Minister Dr Manik Saha launched the new service on Saturday. In the Northeast, improved railway connections did not come easily. Railway services first arrived in Tripura in 1964, but the network did not expand much for many years. Changes began after railway development in the Northeast became a national project. Rail connectivity reached Kumarghat in 1990 and then extended to Agartala in 2008. Now, the region marks another milestone with its first MEMU train. This train will run as 75679/75680 between Agartala and Karimganj junction. A senior railway official said that the train can carry about 3000 passengers, including both seated and standing commuters. NFR Chief Public Relations Officer (CPRO) Kapinjal Kishore Sharma said that MEMU trains can speed up and slow down way too quickly than DEMU trains. This allows them to cover suburban and short-distance routes more effectively. He also mentioned that MEMU trains provide smoother rides, lower noise levels, fewer maintenance needs, and zero emissions at the point of operation. The MEMU train will run six days a week and will complete the 205 km journey in around 6 hours and 30 minutes. This train will stop at 23 stations between Agartala and Karimganj. Stations covered: Jogendra Nagar, Jirnia, Teliamura, Mungiyakami, Ambasa, Jawaharnagar, SK Para, Manu, Nalkata, Kumar Ghat, Pencharthal, Panisagar, Dharmanagar, Nadiapur, Churaibari, Tilbhum, Chandkhira Bagn, Kalkalighat, Patharkandi, Kanaibazar, Baraigram Junction, Kayasthagram, Nilambazar and Suprakan

NEW DELHI (Metro Rail News): The Prayagraj division of the North Central Railway zone has registered a 16.78% increase in commercial revenue earning Rs. 258.38 crore in June 2026, compared to Rs. 221.26 crore of revenue earned in June 2025. The Prayagraj division is a key railway division of the North Central Railway zone. The division also surpassed its targeted commercial revenue of Rs. 241.61 crore by 6.94%. The Prayagraj Division forms the spinal cord of Indian Railways connecting the coal-steel belt of the Eastern region to the consuming areas. Having a total route length of 1070 kms, it encompasses the Southern part of Uttar Pradesh and covers 21 districts. PRO of Prayagraj Division, Amit Kumar Singh said, “Regular intensive ticket-checking campaigns are being carried out at stations and on trains to curb ticketless and irregular travel. At the same time, the division has continued efforts to provide passengers with safe, clean and quality railway services, including better catering, safe drinking water and hygienic toilet facilities. He credited the growth to the Senior Divisional Commercial Manager Hari Mohan and Divisional Railway Manager Rajneesh Agrawal. The revenue growth was led by better ticket-checking, increased freight loading, expansion of non-fare revenue sources, strategic revenue optimisation, and enhanced facilities for the passengers. articles.title: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs
articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs
articles.extracted_text: An official website of the United States Government Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you've safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. The Federal Reserve, the central bank of the United States, provides the nation with a safe, flexible, and stable monetary and financial system. The Federal Reserve Board on Tuesday requested comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs. The amendments are intended to align with changes to anti-money laundering program requirements separately proposed by four other agencies. Among other changes, the proposal would require banks to focus their anti-money laundering resources based on risk, with more attention given to higher-risk customers and activities. The proposed amendments would also require banks to incorporate the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network's anti-money laundering priorities into their risk assessment processes. Under the proposal, once a bank has established an anti-money laundering program, the Federal Reserve would focus supervision and enforcement activities on significant failures to implement the program. Comments on the proposal are due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register . For media inquiries, please email [email protected] or call 202-452-2955. Federal Register notice: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs (PDF)

articles.title: Philip R. Lane: AI and monetary policy
articles.extracted_text: Let me begin by congratulating everyone involved in the ChaMP research network on a remarkably successful research programme: it has delivered many new insights regarding the transmission of monetary policy and has been directly influential in our policy discussions in recent years. In these dinner remarks, I would like to focus on one topic in particular: the implications of artificial intelligence (AI) for the monetary policy stance. [ 2 ] A natural benchmark analysis is to view AI as permanently increasing productivity, boosting incomes. If households and firms quickly internalise the permanent nature of the productivity shock and incorporate future increases in incomes into their spending decisions, the advent and adoption of AI could put upward pressure on inflation via this demand mechanism already early on during the transition phase. [ 3 ] Yet, assuming that households and firms know precisely the nature, size and persistence of future productivity shocks is hardly realistic. A more sluggish consumption response can also be rationalised if the level of lagged consumption is an important determinant of the benefits of current consumption, as in “habit formation” models. [ 4 ] Consumers also face great individual-specific uncertainty about the income implications of the AI transition, providing a further reason to be slow to adjust consumption. [ 5 ] It is more plausible to posit that households and firms will learn about the income and employment impact of productivity shocks over time in a concurrent manner and will only slowly adapt spending to it. [ 6 ] In this event, the upfront inflationary effect would be strongly diminished. More generally, within the span of macroeconomic outcomes originating from different degrees of incorporating productivity and income gains into spending decisions, the inflationary effects of the AI transition will depend on a range of factors. One factor in determining the income, distribution and demand effects is whether the technological boost from AI will be labour-augmenting or capital-augmenting. Technology is often modelled as labour-augmenting: more can be produced with the same number of workers. This effect boosts labour income for workers, with the scale depending on their bargaining power and on institutional factors. Conversely, if AI is capital-augmenting, income increases will accrue to capital owners, rather than workers, thereby increasing inequality of labour and capital income. [ 7 ] An increase in income and wealth inequalities could limit the extent to which demand expands across all sectors of the economy and thereby dampens the inflationary tendencies associated with AI-driven productivity gains. [ 8 ] A second factor is the scale of investment required to integrate AI into the economic value chain. Here, substantial computing power is likely required both in building AI foundational models and in implementing AI in business settings. Building the required computational infrastructure requires a substantial upfront increase in capital expenditures. A third factor is that the expansion in AI-related compute involves a substantial increase in energy demand and, until energy supply catches up, puts upward pressure on energy prices. [ 9 ] This dynamic is likely to add to inflationary pressure during the AI adoption phase. The geographical distribution of AI activity is likely to be relevant for the impact on demand at the regional or national level. If AI activity turns out to rema

articles.title: 2.Purchasing Managersâ Index for June 2026
articles.extracted_text: Department of Service Statistics of NBS of China China Federation of Logistics & Purchasing (CFLP) I. Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the purchasing managersâ index (PMI) of Chinaâs manufacturing industry was 50.3%, an increase of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month and back to the expansionary range. By enterprise size, the PMI for large-sized enterprises was 50.7%, a decrease of 0.4 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for medium sized enterprises was 50.5%, an increase of 1.9 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for small-sized enterprises was 48.2%, a decrease of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month, which was below the threshold. By sub-index, among the five sub-indexes that constitute the manufacturing PMI, the production index and the new order index were both above the threshold, while the raw materials inventory index, the employment index and the supplier delivery time index were all below the threshold. The production index was 51.4%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating an accelerated expansion in production activities in the manufacturing industry. The new order index was 51.2%, an increase of 1.3 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the market demand of the manufacturing industry. The raw materials inventory index was 48.4%, a decrease of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a continued decline in the inventories of main raw materials in the manufacturing industry. The employment index was 48.5%, a decrease of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a slight decline in the employment climate of the manufacturing industry. The supplier delivery time index was 49.9%, an increase of 0.7 percentage points from the previous month, and still below the threshold, indicating a slight slowdown in raw material delivery times among manufacturing suppliers. Non-manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the non-manufacturing business activity index was 50.2%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the climate of non-manufacturing industries. By industry, the business activity index for the construction industry was 49.0%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month; that for the service industry was 50.4%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month. In terms of the service industry, the business activity indexes for telecommunication, radio, television and satellite transmission services, Internet software and information technology services, monetary and financial services and insurance were all in the relatively high climate range of above 55.0%; those for civil aviation transportation and real estate were all below the threshold. The new order index was 48.0%, an increase of 3.0 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a significant improvement in the market demand climate of the non-manufacturing industry. articles.title: 3.Report on the Development of Culture and Related Industries in 2025
articles.extracted_text: In 2025, under the strong leadership of the Party Central Committee with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and plans of the Party Central Committee and the State Council. Centered on the goal of building China into a country with a strong culture, the country moved faster to nurture new-type cultural enterprises and new cultural forms, optimized the business environment, and improved the quality of development. Cultural and related industries achieved vigorous growth with steadily rising profitability. Industries with new cultural forms took the lead in driving development, and scientific and technological innovation in cultural industries picked up speed. i. Business revenue of culture and related industries exceeded 20 trillion yuan With the continuous improvement of Chinaâs cultural industrial system and market system, Chinaâs culture and related industries (hereinafter referred to as âcultural industriesâ) have expanded steadily. In 2025, cultural industries nationwide achieved a business revenue of 20,825.4 billion yuan [1] , surpassing the 20 trillion yuan threshold and hitting a record high, up by 8.8% year on year [2] . Underpinned by culture-related services, the structure of cultural industries kept improving. In 2025, culture-related services achieved a business revenue of 12,305.2 billion yuan, up by 12.8% year on year, accounting for 59.1% of the total business revenue of cultural industries, up by 2.1 percentage points year on year. Culture-related services contributed 82.7% to the business revenue growth of cultural industries. Culture-related manufacturing and culture-related wholesale and retail achieved business revenue of 4,991.9 billion yuan and 3,528.3 billion yuan, respectively, up by 0.6% and 8.0% year on year. ii. Business revenue of nine major sectors of cultural industries achieved growth across the board In 2025, business revenue saw growth across nine major sectors of cultural industries. Specifically, four sectors posted double-digit business revenue growth year on year: creative design services (13.2%), content creation and production (12.4%), news and information services (12.3%), and cultural, entertainment and leisure services (10.7%). The other five sectors recorded year-on-year growth rates as follows: 8.0% in cultural equipment production, 7.7% in cultural communication channels, 3.1% in cultural investment and operation, 2.7% in cultural auxiliary production and intermediary services, and 2.5% in cultural consumption terminal production. By region, cultural industries in eastern and western regions achieved business revenue of 15,528.0 billion yuan and 2,029.8 billion yuan, up by 9.5% and 9.9% year on year, respectively, both higher than the national average growth rate. The central and northeastern regions achieved business revenue of 3,007.1 billion yuan and 260.5 billion yuan, up by 4.7% and 4.8% year on year. i. Total assets of cultural industries kept rising At the end of 2025, the total assets of Chinaâs cultural industries reached 37,447.4 billion yuan, an increase of 8.9% over the end of the previous year. By industry type, total assets of culture-related manufacturing, culture-related wholesale and retail, and culture-related services were 5,060.2 billion yuan, 2,744.3 billion yuan and 29,642.8 billion yuan, respectively, up by 5.7%, 7.2% and 9.6% over the end of the previous year. By field, assets of core areas of culture [

articles.title: 4.Profits of Industrial Enterprises above the Designated Size from January to May in 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total profits of industrial enterprises above the designated size nationwide reached 3,143.96 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 18.8% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 2 for details). From January to May, among industrial enterprises above the designated size, state-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 1,048.66 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 19.6%; share-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 2,434.81 billion yuan, an increase of 24.1%; enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan realized a total profit of 695.72 billion yuan, an increase of 4.2%; private enterprises realized a total profit of 772.65 billion yuan, an increase of 10.7%. From January to May, the mining industry realized a total profit of 479.52 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 33.5%; the manufacturing industry realized a total profit of 2,328.52 billion yuan, an increase of 20.0%; the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water realized a total profit of 335.92 billion yuan, a decrease of 2.7%. From January to May, the profits of main industries were as follows: the profit of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals increased by 117.1% year on year, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment increased by 103.9%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products increased by 71.6%, that of the mining and washing of coal increased by 33.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas increased by 17.2%, that of the textile industry increased by 11.7%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery decreased by 0.2%, that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power decreased by 4.1%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery decreased by 5.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products decreased by 13.3%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus decreased by 13.7%, that of the manufacture of automobiles decreased by 19.8%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals decreased by 37.4%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products decreased by 48.9%, and the processing of petroleum, coal and other fuels turned from loss to profit. From January to May, industrial enterprises above the designated size achieved a business revenue of 56.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.5%; the operating costs incurred were 48.04 trillion yuan, an increase of 4.7%; the profit rate of business revenue was 5.56%, a year-on-year increase of 0.63 percentage points. At the end of May, the assets of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 193.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.8%; the liabilities totaled 112.69 trillion yuan, an increase of 6.5%; the ownersâ equity totaled 80.87 trillion yuan, an increase of 5.0%; the asset-liability ratio was 58.2%, a year-on-year increase of 0.3 percentage points. At the end of May, the accounts receivable of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 28.17 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 7.7%; the inventory of finished goods was 7.14 trillion yuan, an increase of 8.8%. articles.title: 6.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. articles.title: 7.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. articles.title: 8.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: 9.Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 20,603.1 billion yuan, up by 1.4% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 19,002.2 billion yuan, up by 2.7%. In May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 4,109.0 billion yuan, down by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 3,778.1 billion yuan, up by 1.1%. By business area, from January to May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 17,866.2 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; those in rural areas were 2,736.9 billion yuan, up by 2.6%. In May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 3,574.1 billion yuan, down by 0.9% year on year; those in rural areas were 534.9 billion yuan, up by 1.5%. By consumption pattern, from January to May, the retail sales of goods were 18,254.3 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 2,348.8 billion yuan, up by 3.1%. In May, the retail sales of goods were 3,648.5 billion yuan, down by 0.7% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 460.5 billion yuan, up by 0.6%. By retail format, from January to May, the retail sales of convenience stores and supermarkets among retail enterprises (units) above the designated size increased by 6.8% and 3.6% year on year, respectively; those of specialty stores, department stores and brand-exclusive stores decreased by 1.2%, 1.8% and 7.6%, respectively. From January to May, Chinaâs online retail sales of goods and services reached 8,317.7 billion yuan, up by 5.9% year on year. Specifically, the online retail sales of goods were 5,271.8 billion yuan, up by 5.0%; among the online retail sales of goods, those of food, clothing, and daily necessities increased by 15.5%, 7.2%, and 1.6%, respectively. The online retail sales of services were 3,045.9 billion yuan, up by 7.6%. articles.title: 10.Energy Production in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size remained at a relatively high level, the growth of crude oil production was steady, the growth of natural gas production fell slightly, and the growth of electricity generation accelerated. I. Raw Coal, Crude Oil and Natural Gas Production and Related Information Raw coal production remained at a relatively high level. In May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 400 million tons, down by 1.7% year on year; the average daily output was 12.81 million tons. From January to May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1.98 billion tons, a year-on-year decrease of 0.3%. The growth of crude oil production was steady. In May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 18.57 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 0.5%, 0.7 percentage points lower than that in April; the average daily output was 599 thousand tons. From January to May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 91.31 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. The decline in the processing volume of crude oil expanded. In May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 53.72 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 9.1%, with the decline expanded by 3.3 percentage points from April; the average daily processing volume was 1.733 million tons. From January to May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 292.80 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%. Natural gas production fell slightly. In May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 21.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%, compared with a 1.9% increase in April; the average daily output was 700 million cubic meters. From January to May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 111.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year increase of 1.7%. Electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size grew faster. In May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 784.3 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 4.2%, with the growth rate 1.6 percentage points higher than that in April; the average daily electricity generation was 25.30 billion kWh. From January to May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 3,912.9 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 3.6%. By category, in May, the growth of thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size slowed down, while that of hydropower and solar power generation accelerated; the growth of nuclear power and wind power generation turned from decline to growth. Among them, thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 2.1% year on year, with the growth rate 1.0 percentage point lower than that in April; hydropower generation increased by 13.0%, with the growth rate 0.8 percentage points higher than that in April; nuclear power generation increased by 5.0%, compared with an 8.7% decline in April; wind power generation increased by 0.5%, compared with a 5.0% decline in April; solar power generation increased by 12.1%, with the growth rate 5.0 percentage points higher than that i

Service Sector Grew Steadily and Modern Services Showed Good Momentum of Development. articles.title: Nigel Farage tries to ‘take back control’
articles.raw_summary: Why the Reform party leader is quitting as an MP
articles.extracted_text: Roula Khalaf, Editor of the FT, selects her favourite stories in this weekly newsletter. Your browser does not support playing this file but you can still download the MP3 file to play locally. Has Nigel Farage played his trump card by announcing that he will resign as MP for Clacton and fight a by-election there, or will the move backfire? After mounting pressure over donations from wealthy backers, he insisted he has “done nothing wrong” and that he would fight “the establishment” to retake the seat for Reform UK. But it might not be the contest he anticipated. In this breaking news episode, deputy opinion editor Miranda Green is joined by chief political commentator Robert Shrimsley and UK political editor George Parker to examine what the move means for Reform, the scrutiny of Farage’s finances and the future of his political career. As it happened: Nigel Farage quits as MP to force Clacton by-election Parliamentary watchdog urged to investigate Nigel Farage over benefits from fraudster Nigel Farage to make statement on his ‘future in public life’ Farage struggles to be ringmaster of his own circus Sign up here for Stephen Bush’s morning newsletter Inside Politics for straight-talking insight into the stories that matter, plus puns and tongue (mostly) in cheek views. articles.title: Meeting with Director General of the Russian Export Centre Veronika Nikishina
articles.raw_summary: The President held a working meeting with Director General of the Russian Export Centre (REC, VEB.RF Group). Veronika Nikishina reported on the increase of Russia's non-commodity non-energy exports, the establishment of regional export support centres, and the development of stable partnerships with friendly nations.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International The President held a working meeting with Director General of the Russian Export Centre (REC, VEB.RF Group). Veronika Nikishina reported on the increase of Russia's non-commodity non-energy exports, the establishment of regional export support centres, and the development of stable partnerships with friendly nations. President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Ms Nikishina, we are aware of all the subjective and objective difficulties facing our exporters. Nevertheless, the volume of non-resource non-energy exports increased by 9 percent in 2025 compared to 2024, thanks to support from the Export Centre, the entire group of the Export Centre. Overall, it reached 1.7 trillion rubles. Director General of the Russian Export Centre Veronika Nikishina: Yes, 1.7 trillion rubles. Vladimir Putin: This is a considerable amount. Veronika Nikishina: Mr President, it has been said recently at some platforms that we do not need export because it allegedly moves the commodities we need at home to foreign markets, excessively strengthening the ruble. We think this view is fundamentally wrong. First, no country can be successful on the international stage without maintaining a strong position in the sphere of non-resource export. We can see that a tough competition is unfolding for economic influence and leadership, and non-resource export is an instrument of international cooperation and a means of creating stable partnerships and alliances. Second, export – both resource and non-resource – amid slowing economic growth is also a means of stabilising the situation and resuming economic growth, because this implies additional markets that bring revenues and tax deductions, prevent lay-offs and help preserve jobs and even create new ones. Incidentally, our geopolitical opponents, who have been imposing sanctions against Russian exports since 2022, trying to push us from foreign markets as much as possible and hence weaken us economically, are also aware of that. But we have fundamentally achieved our key goal, which is to quickly shift our focus towards the friendly markets. We have maintained our export volumes and reached a stage of sustainable growth, as you have absolutely correctly noted. Last year, our non-resource exports increased by 11 percent, mechanical engineering exports by 28 percent, and the share of friendly countries in our structure has reached 86 percent. Our current priority is to attain national development goals which you have set for the Government, that is, to increase exports by two-thirds by 2030 compared to 2023. Vladimir Putin (Looking at the presentation.) : I see that the development of stable partnerships with friendly countries is proceeding apace. Veronika Nikishina: Yes, we believe we have completed the process of switching to foreign markets. We have restored the pre-sanction export volumes and have started to increase exports by forming stable partnerships and alliances with friendly countries, because our non-resource export constitutes the basis of our partnerships, including technological ones. We are also a reliable supplier of food and commodities that form value chains. We sincerely believe that the trend outlined at the third stage is not only realistic but that we are also perfectly on schedule.
